title: Employer of Record Services in Vietnam
metaDescription: Recruit both local and foreign employees for your company without establishing a legal entity in Vietnam with an employer of record (EOR) agency.

hero.title: Employer of Record Services in Vietnam
hero.subtitle: Employer of Record (EOR) services allow you to hire employees in Vietnam without establishing a legal entity. Professional Employer Organization (PEO) allows you to outsource all recruitment, payroll, and legal compliance needs so you can focus on your company's bottom line.

sections.heading: Employer of Record (EOR) with InCorp Vietnam
body: Initial Tasks & Implementation: Our all-inclusive HR services ensure compliance and efficiency. We conduct thorough KYC checks, gather employee data, create compliant contracts, facilitate signings, and manage payroll and insurance registration for seamless HR operations. The service helps you: perform KYC of client; collect employee's salary package, contract terms from the client; interview and perform standard background check of employee; prepare and finalize all required HR contracts in compliance with Vietnam labor law; meet with employees and sign contracts with employees; prepare payroll document template and send it to the client.

Initial Labor Registration: InCorp Vietnam manages vital labor aspects, registering employees, ensuring local compliance, and structuring salary scales tailored to organizational positions for efficient HR management. The service helps you: Labor Report Registration (to register employee with company); Company Regulation registered with the local authorities; salary scale set up.

Initial Social Insurance Registration: InCorp Vietnam offers vital documentation for HR and insurance management, including delivery documents, declaration sheets, and employee information and labor lists. The service helps you: delivery documents sheet; participating and adjusting declaration sheet on Social, Health and Unemployment Insurance for each employee; list of Employee Information and Company Labor List.

Monthly and Quarterly Tasks: InCorp Vietnam offers vital documentation for HR and insurance management, including delivery documents, declaration sheets, and employee information and labor lists. The service helps you: monthly, based on client timesheet, annual leave and basic employee information reports to do monthly payroll and business trip reimbursement (if any); send payslip and create salary payment order; collect and provide client employee PIT information for the accounting department; calculate, withhold and settle monthly/quarterly PIT for EOR employees.

Outsourcing Service: InCorp Vietnam offers a range of services for seamless HR management, including monthly salary transfers, payroll processing based on employee reports, and dispute resolution facilitation. The service helps you: monthly transfer of employee salary from HR Techsoft bank account to employee bank account, based on client timesheets, annual leave and basic employee information reports to do monthly payroll; act as a mediator between employee and the Client to negotiate, settle labor disputes (if any).

sections.heading: What to Know about Vietnam's Labor Rules
body: Working Hours: Regular working hours: 8 hours/day or 48 hours/week. Employers choose daily or weekly hours, but daily not over 10. Weekly hours should not exceed 48, with a 40-hour option encouraged by the State.

National Holidays: All Employees shall be entitled to fully paid days off on the following public holidays: Gregorian Calendar New Year Holiday: 01 day (the 1st of January of the Gregorian calendar); Lunar New Year (Tet Holiday): 05 days, decided by the Prime Minister; Victory Day: 30th of April; International Labor Day: May 1st; National Day: 02 days off (the 2nd of September and the previous or next day). Foreign employees in Vietnam are additionally entitled to 01 traditional public holiday and 01 National Day of their country, in addition to the public holidays stipulated above.

Tet Holiday (Lunar New Year) Days off & Bonus: The Tet falls at the beginning of the Lunar Calendar, and most businesses take 5 days off for this holiday. The exact days are decided by the Prime Minister of Vietnam. Tet holiday can be a disruptive time for a company's workforce. This is the most important holiday of the year and often times employees will take extra days off. Furthermore, the turnover rate during this time tends to increase significantly. It is not required but it is expected to pay Vietnamese employees a special bonus for the Tet holiday, usually calculated as the 13th month's salary.

sections.heading: Frequently Asked Questions on Employer of Record Services in Vietnam

items.q: Do I need a legal entity in Vietnam to hire employees?
items.a: No. You can recruit both local and foreign employees in Vietnam for your company without establishing a legal entity. Common ways to do this include using recruitment technology platforms, employer of record (EOR) providers, or direct hiring through various communication channels such as LinkedIn and Facebook.

items.q: Can I pay my employees from abroad as a foreign employer?
items.a: Yes. You can set up your payroll and payroll compliance entirely remotely. As legal obligations, you have to submit all paperwork for employee registration and salary payment to relevant authorities, namely the Department or Ministry of Labour, Invalids and Social Affairs.

items.q: What are all the services included in the HR outsourcing package?
items.a: When applying for our comprehensive HR outsourcing services, you can choose one, some, or all of the following: initial tasks and implementation, initial labor registration, initial social insurance registration, monthly and quarterly tasks, and outsourcing service.
